sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 67-year-old male who presents with a complaint of right shoulder pain, which started approximately 4 days ago after being tackled during a game of tackle football.
He reports that he has been experiencing pain in the back of the right shoulder, specifically around the acromioclavicular joint, and has difficulty lifting heavy objects due to pain.
He denies any numbness or tingling in his fingers and has not had any fever or chills.
He has tried taking Tylenol for the pain but has not found it to be very effective.
He denies any neck problems or other joint issues.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of appendicitis, for which he underwent an appendectomy approximately 3-4 years ago.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms or problems.
Current medications: The patient is taking Tylenol for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 122/76 mmHg, heart rate is 82 beats per minute, and oxygen saturation is 95% on room air.
Physical exam: The cervical spine exam is unremarkable, with no tenderness to palpation.

The right shoulder exam reveals pain to palpation of the posterior shoulder at the acromioclavicular joint, decreased abduction and adduction to 90 degrees, pain with supination and pronation of the right arm, and a palpable radial artery pulse.
Test Results: The patient's right shoulder x-ray shows an acute acromioclavicular joint fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an acute acromioclavicular joint fracture.
Problem list: Acromioclavicular joint fracture; status: confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day for pain management.
I ordered a CT scan of the right shoulder to further evaluate the joint.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the shoulder and avoid potential future surgery.
I instructed the patient to wear a sling to support the shoulder.
Follow-up: I advised the patient to follow up in 1-2 weeks to re-evaluate the fracture and determine the need for further treatment.
